search:Kubernetes + Redis Advanced Confi.content:
- Deploy Redis using Helm: `helm install my-redis bitnami/redis`
- Use ConfigMap for configuration: 
  ```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: redis-config
data:
  redis.conf: |
    maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
  ```
- Mount ConfigMap in Redis Pod:
  ```yaml
volumeMounts:
  - name: config-volume
    mountPath: /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf
    subPath: redis.conf
volumes:
  - name: config-volume
    configMap:
      name: redis-config
  ```
- Deploy Redis Cluster with Helm: `helm install my-redis-cluster bitnami/redis --set cluster.enabled=true`
- Use Redis Sentinel for high availability: 
  ```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis-sentinel
spec:
  serviceName: "sentinel"
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis-sentinel
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: redis-sentinel
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: sentinel
        image: bitnami/redis-sentinel
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26379
        env:
        - name: REDIS_MASTER_HOST
          value: "my-redis-master"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: config-volume
          mountPath: /usr/local/etc/redis/sentinel.conf
          subPath: sentinel.conf
volumes:
  - name: config-volume
    configMap:
      name: redis-sentinel-config
  ```
- Latest Helm version: `helm version --short`
- Key Config Options:
  - `maxmemory`: Maximum memory usage for Redis instance.
  - `cluster-enabled`: Enable Redis Cluster mode.
  - `sentinel monitor`: Monitor a master and take action when it goes down.

- Deploy Redis Cluster using Helm: `helm install my-redis bitnami/redis --set cluster.enabled=true`
- Use ConfigMap for Redis configuration:
  ```yaml
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: ConfigMap
  metadata:
    name: redis-config
  data:
    redis.conf: |
      maxmemory 2gb
      maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
  ```
- Apply ConfigMap to your deployment:
  ```bash
  kubectl apply -f redis-config.yaml
  ```
- Create a StatefulSet for Redis:
  ```yaml
  apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: StatefulSet
  metadata:
    name: redis
  spec:
    serviceName: "redis"
    replicas: 3
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: redis
    template:
      metadata:
        labels:
          app: redis
      spec:
        containers:
        - name: redis
          image: bitnami/redis:latest
          ports:
          - containerPort: 6379
            name: redis
          volumeMounts:
          - name: redis-config-volume
            mountPath: /opt/bitnami/redis/conf/redis.conf
            subPath: redis.conf
        volumes:
        - name: redis-config-volume
          configMap:
            name: redis-config
  ```
- Expose Redis Cluster using a Headless Service:
  ```yaml
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: Service
  metadata:
    name: redis
  spec:
    ports:
      - port: 6379
        targetPort: 6379
    clusterIP: None
    selector:
      app: redis
  ```
- Access Redis Cluster from another pod:
  ```bash
  kubectl run -i --tty --rm debug --image=busybox --restart=Never -- nslookup redis-0.redis.default.svc.cluster.local
  ```

Note: Replace `my-redis`, `redis-config.yaml`, and other placeholders with your actual values.

- Use `kubectl apply -f <filename>.yaml` to deploy Redis on Kubernetes.
- For high availability, use Redis Sentinel or Redis Cluster.
- Install Redis Enterprise Operator using Helm with the command:
  ```bash
  helm install my-release bitnami/redis-enterprise --set sentinel.enabled=true
  ```
- Configure Redis using a ConfigMap by creating a `config.yaml` file and applying it with:
  ```bash
  kubectl apply -f config.yaml
  ```
- Ensure your Kubernetes cluster has at least two nodes for redundancy.
- Verify the deployment status with `kubectl get pods`.
- For automatic failover, use Redis Sentinel or Redis Cluster configurations.
- Update Helm charts to the latest version using `helm upgrade my-release bitnami/redis-enterprise`.

- Install Redis using `sudo apt-get install redis-server`
- Configure Redis memory usage with `maxmemory` flag in the configuration file.
- Enable persistence with `save` directives to define when data should be written to disk.
- Set up replication by configuring `slaveof <master-ip> <port>` on slave nodes.
- Adjust security settings with `bind`, `requirepass`, and `protected-mode`.
- Optimize performance with `maxclients`, `hz`, and `tcp-keepalive` flags.

- **CLI Commands**:
  - `redis-cli --version` to check the latest version.
  - `redis-server /path/to/redis.conf` to start Redis with a custom configuration file.

- **Key Config Options**:
  - `maxmemory`: Sets the maximum memory usage for Redis.
  - `maxmemory-policy`: Defines the eviction policy when maxmemory is reached.
  - `bind`: Specifies the IP addresses that the server will listen on.
  - `protected-mode`: Enables or disables protected mode.
  - `requirepass`: Sets a password to access the Redis server.

- **Latest Version**:
  - As of my last update, the latest stable version of Redis is 7.0.5.
